data.narrative:
Planet Labs is a publicly traded Space & Aerospace company founded by Will Marshall, Robbie Schingler, and Chris Boshuizen, currently valued at $7B+ after raising $700M+. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 3 same-sector public comparables: Rocket Lab ($37.0B), Firefly Aerospace ($6.3B), and Astroscale ($900M).

The peer-median method, the sole applicable triangulation approach, yields a $6.3B implied valuation based on the median of the three public Space & Aerospace companies at similar stage. This method anchors the analysis but must be interpreted within the wide dispersion observed across the comp set, which ranges from $900M to $37.0B. A public-comp anchoring approach using the sector's 45.3x median Price-to-Sales multiple across 6 tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) could not be applied due to the absence of Planet Labs' revenue on file.

The single-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $5.4B to $7.2B, with $6.3B as the central estimate. The stated $7B+ valuation sits at the upper end of this derived range. Key uncertainties include the wide scatter in the peer comp set—a 41x spread between minimum and maximum—and limited insight into revenue-based multiples given the target's public status and the sector's historically variable P/S multiples. Additional revenue or profitability data would strengthen conviction in the valuation range.
